description: MVP Phases 0.5–5: CI/CD, test infrastructure, stock & fulfillment, financial engine, Mercado Livre integration, post-sale messaging, LGPD compliance, security hardening, and production deployment.

userStories.title: GitHub Actions CI Pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated build and test checks on every PR so regressions are caught before merge.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/ci.yml` created
Backend job: `dotnet restore` → `dotnet build` → `dotnet test` (all test projects)
Frontend job: `npm ci` → `ng build --configuration=production` → `ng test --watch=false --browsers=ChromeHeadless`
Both jobs run in parallel on `push` to `main` and on all PRs
Pipeline fails if any step fails
Badge in README shows CI status
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Phase 0.5 — DevOps & Quality Foundation. No dependencies.

userStories.title: Docker Image Build & Push Pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Docker images built and pushed automatically so deployments are reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/docker.yml` created
Triggers on push to `main` (not on PRs)
Builds `Dockerfile.api`, `Dockerfile.worker`, `Dockerfile.web` images
Pushes to GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io)
Tags images with commit SHA and `latest`
Uses Docker layer caching for faster builds
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Phase 0.5. Depends on US-001 (CI before Docker push).

userStories.title: Test Infrastructure Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want test projects configured with TestContainers so integration tests run against real PostgreSQL and Redis.
acceptanceCriteria: `tests/PeruShopHub.UnitTests/` project created with xUnit + FluentAssertions + Moq
`tests/PeruShopHub.IntegrationTests/` project created with xUnit + TestContainers
`IntegrationTestBase` class spins up PostgreSQL 16 + Redis 7 containers
`CustomWebApplicationFactory<Program>` uses TestContainers connection strings
Database migrated automatically in test setup
Both test projects added to CI pipeline
`dotnet test` runs successfully from repo root
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Phase 0.5. Foundation for all test stories.

userStories.title: Unit Tests — Financial Calculations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests covering all financial calculation logic so cost decomposition is provably correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests for `CostCalculationService.CalculateOrderCostsAsync` — verifies product_cost, packaging, commission, fixed_fee, tax categories
Tests for commission resolution: specific match → category fallback → default fallback → hardcoded 13%
Tests for weighted average cost on PO receive
Tests for cost distribution: `by_value` and `by_quantity`
Tests for `RecalculateOrderCostsAsync` — preserves manual/API costs
Tests for profit: `TotalAmount - Sum(Costs)`
Edge cases: zero quantity, zero price, missing variant, missing commission rule
All tests pass, minimum 15 test cases
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Unit Tests — ProductService & OrderService
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for ProductService and OrderService so core business logic is verified.
acceptanceCriteria: ProductService tests: create (SKU auto-gen), update, delete, category filtering, search
OrderService tests: list with pagination, detail, fulfillment flow, timeline building
Mock dependencies (DbContext, ICacheService) using Moq
All tests pass, minimum 12 test cases
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Phase 0.5. Split from original US-005. Depends on US-003.

userStories.title: Unit Tests — CategoryService, PurchaseOrderService, InventoryService
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for CategoryService, PurchaseOrderService, and InventoryService so remaining core services are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: CategoryService tests: hierarchy traversal, circular reference detection, slug uniqueness
PurchaseOrderService tests: create, receive, cost allocation, status gating
InventoryService tests: overview calculation, movement creation, stock adjustment
Mock dependencies using Moq
All tests pass, minimum 18 test cases
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Phase 0.5. Split from original US-005. Depends on US-003.

userStories.title: Integration Tests — Controllers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests hitting real endpoints with a real database so API contracts are verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Auth flow test: register → login → get token → access protected endpoint
Product CRUD test: create → read → update → delete → verify 404
Order flow test: create order → add costs → fulfill → verify stock decreased
Category test: create parent → create child → verify hierarchy
Tenant isolation test: Tenant A data invisible to Tenant B
Concurrency test: two simultaneous updates → one succeeds, one gets 409
Tests use `CustomWebApplicationFactory` with TestContainers
All tests pass
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Angular Test Setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Angular unit tests for shared components and critical services so frontend regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Karma/Jasmine configured (or Jest)
Tests for `AuthService`: login, register, token storage, refresh, logout
Tests for `DataGridComponent`: pagination, sorting, search, empty state
Tests for `FormFieldComponent`: validation display, error messages
Tests for currency formatting (BRL, monospace)
Tests run in CI with `ng test --watch=false --browsers=ChromeHeadless`
Minimum 15 test cases
Angular build passes

userStories.title: API Rate Limiting per Tenant
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want per-tenant rate limiting so one tenant cannot overload the system.
acceptanceCriteria: .NET built-in rate limiter or `AspNetCoreRateLimit` configured
Rate limit: 100 requests/minute per tenant (configurable via appsettings)
Response headers: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, `X-RateLimit-Reset`
429 Too Many Requests with `Retry-After` header when exceeded
Super-admin exempt from rate limiting
Rate limit counters in Redis (shared across instances)
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Structured Logging with Serilog
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want structured JSON logging with request correlation for debugging production issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Serilog packages: `Serilog.AspNetCore`, `Serilog.Sinks.Console`, `Serilog.Sinks.File`
JSON format for all log output
CorrelationId per request (from header or auto-generated)
Log entries include: CorrelationId, TenantId, UserId, Endpoint, StatusCode, ElapsedMs
Request/response logging middleware (configurable)
Log levels configurable per namespace
Sensitive data excluded (passwords, tokens)
Log files rotated daily, 30 days retention
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Error Tracking with Sentry
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want unhandled exceptions reported to Sentry for immediate alerting.
acceptanceCriteria: `Sentry.AspNetCore` NuGet package added
DSN configurable via `SENTRY_DSN` env var
Unhandled exceptions auto-reported with stack trace
Request context: URL, method, sanitized headers, user ID, tenant ID
GlobalExceptionFilter sends to Sentry before returning error
Environment tag: development/staging/production
Disabled when DSN not configured (local dev)
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Health Check Dashboard
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want health check endpoints verifying all dependencies for monitoring.
acceptanceCriteria: `/health` returns aggregated status (Healthy/Degraded/Unhealthy)
`/health/ready` checks: PostgreSQL, Redis, disk space
`/health/live` returns 200 if process running (liveness probe)
Each check has 5s timeout
Response includes individual check results with duration
Health check UI at `/health-ui`
Checks run every 30 seconds (configurable)
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Phase 0.5 Review — DevOps & Quality Foundation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to review Phase 0.5 implementation for completeness and gaps before starting Phase 1 (Stock & Fulfillment).
acceptanceCriteria: Review all Phase 0.5 stories — verify each acceptance criterion is actually met (not just marked as passing)
Run full build: dotnet build + ng build — zero errors
Run all existing tests — zero failures
Check for missing API endpoints: compare PRD acceptance criteria against actual controllers/routes
Check for missing frontend wiring: verify all new backend endpoints have corresponding frontend service methods and UI elements
Check for missing database migrations: verify all new entities/fields have migrations that apply cleanly
Check for incomplete validation: verify backend validates all required fields and returns proper error responses
Check for missing error handling: verify new features handle loading states, error states, and empty states in the UI
Document any gaps found in progress.txt with specific story IDs and missing criteria
If gaps found: create a list of specific fixes needed (do NOT fix them in this story — just document)
.NET build passes
Angular build passes
userStories.notes: Gate review between Phase 0.5 and Phase 1 (Stock & Fulfillment). If gaps are found, document them and they will be addressed before moving to the next phase.

userStories.title: Purchase Order Receive → Stock Adjustment Flow
userStories.description: As a seller, I want receiving a PO to automatically update stock and product costs.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /api/purchase-orders/{id}/receive` triggers full receive flow
For each PO item: increment `ProductVariant.Stock` by received quantity
Weighted average cost recalculated: (currentStock * currentCost + newQty * newUnitCost) / totalQty
StockMovement created for each item with type 'Entrada', linked to PO
ProductCostHistory record created for each cost change
Additional PO costs distributed proportionally before calculation
Product.PurchaseCost updated as weighted average across active variants
PO status changes to 'Recebido' — cannot be received twice
SignalR notification sent to tenant users
Frontend PO detail shows 'Receber' button (only when status is 'Rascunho')
.NET build passes
Angular build passes

userStories.title: Stock Allocation by Channel — Backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the stock allocation entity and API endpoints so each channel has defined quantities.
acceptanceCriteria: New entity `StockAllocation`: ProductVariantId, MarketplaceId (string), AllocatedQuantity, ReservedQuantity
Migration adds `stock_allocations` table with unique constraint on (VariantId, MarketplaceId, TenantId)
`GET /api/inventory/{productId}/allocations` returns allocations per variant per channel
`PUT /api/inventory/{variantId}/allocations` updates allocation for a marketplace
Validation: sum of allocations ≤ variant total stock
'Unallocated' quantity = total stock - sum(allocations)
When stock changes, allocations proportionally adjusted if exceeding new total
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Stock Allocation by Channel — Frontend
userStories.description: As a seller, I want to see and edit stock allocations per marketplace in the inventory detail view.
acceptanceCriteria: Inventory detail view shows allocation table per marketplace with editable quantities
Unallocated quantity displayed prominently
Inline editing with save button per marketplace row
Validation: cannot allocate more than total stock
Angular build passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Min/Max Stock Rules and Alerts
userStories.description: As a seller, I want configurable minimum/maximum stock levels with alerts before running out.
acceptanceCriteria: New fields on Product: MinStock (int?, nullable), MaxStock (int?, nullable)
Migration adds columns
Product create/edit form includes min/max stock fields
StockAlertWorker (runs every 15min) checks variant stock ≤ product MinStock → creates Notification
Notification type: 'low_stock', includes product name, current stock, min threshold
`GET /api/inventory/alerts` returns products below minimum stock
Dashboard 'Pending Actions' shows low-stock alert count
Inventory overview highlights rows where stock ≤ MinStock in red
No duplicate alert if unread alert exists for same product
.NET build passes
Angular build passes

userStories.title: Stock Movement History with Full Traceability
userStories.description: As a seller, I want a complete audit trail of every stock change with who, when, and why.
acceptanceCriteria: StockMovement includes: CreatedBy (user name/email), Reason (required for adjustments)
Every stock change creates a movement: Entrada, Saida, Ajuste, Reconciliacao
`GET /api/inventory/movements` supports filters: productId, variantId, type, dateFrom, dateTo, createdBy
Paginated, sorted by date descending
Each movement links to source: PurchaseOrderId or OrderId (clickable in UI)
Frontend Movimentacoes tab with filters and data grid
Export to Excel (ClosedXML) button on movements page
Movement reason required for manual adjustments (frontend + backend validation)
.NET build passes
Angular build passes

userStories.title: Internal Stock Reconciliation
userStories.description: As a seller, I want to perform physical stock counts and reconcile differences.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /api/inventory/reconciliation` accepts list of { variantId, countedQuantity }
Compares countedQuantity vs variant.Stock, creates StockMovement type 'Ajuste' with reason 'Reconciliacao fisica'
Updates variant.Stock to counted quantity
Returns report: items checked, discrepancies, total difference
Frontend: Reconciliacao page/dialog with product list, counted qty inputs, current qty shown
Differences highlighted (red if counted < system, yellow if counted > system)
Single batch record links all movements
.NET build passes
Angular build passes

userStories.title: Product Cost History with Effective Dates
userStories.description: As a seller, I want to see how product costs have changed over time for auditing profitability.
acceptanceCriteria: ProductCostHistory uses CreatedAt as effective date
`GET /api/products/{id}/cost-history` returns history sorted by date descending
Response: date, previousCost, newCost, quantity, unitCostPaid, purchaseOrderId, reason
Line chart on Product Detail: cost over time (x=date, y=cost)
Old order cost recalculation uses cost effective at order time
Frontend Product detail 'Historico de Custos' section with table + chart
.NET build passes
Angular build passes

userStories.title: Packaging and Storage Cost per Product
userStories.description: As a seller, I want packaging and daily storage cost per product included in profitability.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify Product.PackagingCost is used in cost calculations
New field: Product.StorageCostDaily (decimal?, nullable)
Migration adds StorageCostDaily column
Product form: 'Custo de embalagem (R$)' and 'Custo de armazenagem diario (R$)' fields
CostCalculationService includes packaging cost in order cost decomposition
Storage calculation: StorageCostDaily × averageDaysInStorage (configurable default: 30)
Storage cost added as OrderCost category 'storage_daily'
.NET build passes
Angular build passes
userStories.notes: Phase 1. Feeds into Phase 2 cost engine.

userStories.title: Commission Engine — Category, Reputation, Listing Type
userStories.description: As a seller, I want commission rates varying by ML category, listing type, and reputation.
acceptanceCriteria: CommissionRule entity supports: MarketplaceId, CategoryPattern, ListingType, Rate, IsDefault
Seed data: ML default commission rules for major categories
Resolution: exact match → category only → marketplace default → hardcoded 13%
GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/settings/commission-rules CRUD
Frontend Settings > Comissoes page with rules table + add/edit/delete
ML Billing API commission overrides calculated value (Source='API' > 'Calculated')
Unit test: resolution algorithm with all fallback levels
.NET build passes
Angular build passes

userStories.title: Tax Calculation Engine
userStories.description: As a seller, I want taxes calculated based on my tax regime for real tax impact per sale.
acceptanceCriteria: New entity TaxProfile: TenantId, TaxRegime (SimplesNacional/LucroPresumido/MEI), AliquotPercentage, State
Migration adds tax_profiles table
GET/PUT /api/settings/tax-profile to read/update tenant's tax profile
Default: Simples Nacional, 6%
CostCalculationService uses tenant's tax rate instead of hardcoded CostSettings:TaxRate
Tax cost as OrderCost category 'tax' with Source 'Calculated'
Frontend Settings > Fiscal page with regime selector and aliquot input
.NET build passes
Angular build passes

userStories.title: Payment Fee Calculation
userStories.description: As a seller, I want payment processing fees calculated per order based on installment count.
acceptanceCriteria: New field on Order: InstallmentCount (int, default 1)
PaymentFeeRule entity: InstallmentRange (min/max), FeePercentage
Seed data: ML default payment fees (1x: 4.99%, etc.)
CostCalculationService calculates payment fee from installment count + total amount
Payment fee as OrderCost category 'payment_fee' with Source 'Calculated'
ML Billing API payment fee replaces calculated value
Frontend Settings: payment fee rules table
Order detail shows installment count and payment fee
.NET build passes
Angular build passes

userStories.title: Complete Cost Composition Service
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CostCalculationService to produce all 10 cost categories with correct sources.
acceptanceCriteria: CalculateOrderCostsAsync produces: product_cost, packaging, marketplace_commission, fixed_fee, tax, payment_fee, shipping_seller, fulfillment_fee, storage_daily, advertising
Each cost has correct Source: 'Calculated', 'API', or 'Manual'
Order.Profit = TotalAmount - Sum(all costs)
Costs with value 0 stored but flagged
.NET build passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2. Depends on US-019, US-020, US-021.

userStories.title: Cost Composition Frontend — Order Detail Breakdown

acceptanceCriteria: Order Detail page: cost breakdown table showing all categories with values and percentages
Cost breakdown pie chart on order detail
Costs with value 0 not shown in UI
Each cost line shows source badge ('API', 'Calculado', 'Manual')

userStories.title: Materialized View — SKU Profitability

EF Core migration creates the materialized view
`REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY` in background worker (every 1 hour)
`GET /api/finance/sku-profitability` reads from materialized view
Supports filters: dateFrom, dateTo, minMargin, maxMargin, search
Supports sorting by any column
Frontend Finance 'Lucratividade por SKU' tab uses this endpoint

GET /api/reports/orders/pdf?dateFrom=&dateTo= returns PDF

userStories.title: Automated Reports by Email

userStories.title: Target-Margin Pricing Rules
userStories.description: As a seller, I want to set a desired margin and get the required selling price calculated.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/pricing/calculate: productId, targetMarginPercent, marketplaceId, listingType
Backwards calculation: Price = ProductCost / (1 - targetMargin - commission - tax - paymentFee - fixedFee)
Returns: suggestedPrice, cost breakdown at that price, actual margin
GET/POST /api/pricing/rules: saved rules per product per marketplace
Frontend Product detail > 'Precificacao' tab with margin input, marketplace selector, calculated price
Bar chart showing price composition (cost, commission, tax, fees, profit)

Current margin vs simulated margin with color diff (green=better, red=worse)

userStories.title: MercadoLivreAdapter — Core Implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the MercadoLivreAdapter implementing IMarketplaceAdapter via DI keyed services.
acceptanceCriteria: MercadoLivreAdapter implements IMarketplaceAdapter in Infrastructure project
Registered as services.AddKeyedScoped<IMarketplaceAdapter, MercadoLivreAdapter>('mercadolivre')
HttpClientFactory with named client 'MercadoLivre', base URL https://api.mercadolibre.com
All methods use CancellationToken
System.Text.Json with camelCase naming
Error handling: deserializes ML error responses
Logging: all API calls logged with correlation ID, endpoint, status code, elapsed time
.NET build passes

userStories.title: OAuth 2.0 Flow with PKCE
userStories.description: As a seller, I want to connect my ML account via OAuth securely.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/integrations/mercadolivre/auth-url generates URL with PKCE (code_challenge, state)
State stored in Redis (5 min TTL) for CSRF prevention
GET /api/integrations/mercadolivre/callback exchanges code for tokens
Tokens stored in MarketplaceConnection entity
AccessToken and RefreshToken encrypted at rest (IDataProtectionProvider)
Connection status 'Active' with TokenExpiresAt
ML user_id stored
Frontend Settings > Integracoes: 'Conectar Mercado Livre' button triggers OAuth
After connection: shows status, ML username, expiration
.NET build passes
Angular build passes

userStories.title: Token Encryption at Rest
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want OAuth tokens encrypted in the database.
acceptanceCriteria: ITokenEncryptionService: Encrypt(plainText), Decrypt(cipherText)
Uses IDataProtectionProvider with purpose 'MarketplaceTokens'
Data protection keys stored in dedicated directory (configurable)
MarketplaceConnection stores encrypted tokens, decrypts on read
Key rotation supported (old keys can still decrypt)
Unit test: encrypt → decrypt roundtrip
Integration test: save connection → reload → token correct
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Token Renewal Background Worker
userStories.description: As a seller, I want ML tokens renewed automatically before expiry.
acceptanceCriteria: TokenRenewalWorker runs every 15 minutes
Queries connections where TokenExpiresAt < now + 30 minutes and status 'Active'
Calls POST /oauth/token with grant_type=refresh_token
Success: updates tokens, resets RefreshErrorCount to 0
Failure: increments RefreshErrorCount
Circuit breaker: after 3 failures, status='Error', notification created
Notification: 'Sua conexao com o Mercado Livre precisa ser reconectada'
All attempts logged with tenant context
.NET build passes

userStories.title: HTTP Client — Circuit Breaker & Rate Limiter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Polly circuit breaker and rate limiting on ML API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Polly packages added (Polly v8 ResiliencePipeline API)
Policy chain: Rate Limiter → Retry → Circuit Breaker
Rate limiter: 300 requests/minute sliding window
Retry: 3 retries, exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s), only 5xx/timeout
Circuit breaker: opens after 5 failures, 30s open, then half-open
Open circuit: immediate failure, logs warning
Rate limit state in-memory (single-instance MVP)
.NET build passes

userStories.title: ML Integration Settings Page
userStories.description: As a seller, I want a settings page showing ML connection status with connect/disconnect.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend: Settings > Integracoes > Mercado Livre
Status badges: Ativo (green), Erro (red), Desconectado (gray)
When connected: seller name, user ID, token expiry, last sync
'Conectar' button → OAuth flow (when disconnected)
'Reconectar' button → OAuth flow (when error)

userStories.title: Security Tests — Tenant Isolation & Auth
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want automated security tests for tenant isolation and auth.
acceptanceCriteria: Tenant A product invisible to Tenant B
Tenant A order → Tenant B GET → 404
Expired JWT → 401 on all endpoints
JWT without tenant → 403 on tenant-required endpoints
Non-admin → admin endpoints → 403
SQL injection in search params → properly escaped
XSS payload in product name → stored safely, rendered escaped
CSRF validation on state-changing endpoints
All pass in CI
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Automated Deploy to VPS
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want automated deployment to production VPS.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/deploy.yml created
Triggers: manual dispatch or push with tag v*
Steps: build → push GHCR → SSH to VPS → pull → docker compose up -d
Zero-downtime: pull new → stop old → start new
Env vars via .env on VPS (not in repo)
Post-deploy health check: curl /health, fail if unhealthy
Rollback documented
SSL/TLS via Let's Encrypt on Nginx
.NET build passes

userStories.title: Automated PostgreSQL Backup
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want automated daily database backups.
acceptanceCriteria: Backup script: pg_dump with compression, daily at 3 AM (cron)
Stored locally + synced to offsite (S3-compatible or Backblaze B2)
Retention: 7 daily, 4 weekly, 3 monthly
Weekly restore verification (automated)
Alert if backup hasn't run in 25 hours
Recovery procedure documented
Script tested: dump → restore → verify integrity

userStories.title: Production Monitoring
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want monitoring for uptime, errors, and performance.
acceptanceCriteria: External uptime ping on /health every 60s (UptimeRobot or similar)
Alert channels: email + optionally Telegram/Slack
Sentry dashboard with alert rules
Nginx access/error logs with rotation
Disk space alert at 80%
Redis memory alert at 80%
PostgreSQL slow query log (> 1s)
Basic /health-ui or Grafana dashboard

userStories.title: Security Review & Hardening
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want OWASP top 10 security review.
acceptanceCriteria: SQL injection review: all queries parameterized (verify no raw SQL without params)
XSS review: Angular sanitizes, backend encodes
CSRF: anti-forgery or SameSite cookies
JWT secret 256+ bits, in env var
HTTPS enforced with HSTS
CORS: only frontend origin
Auth rate limiting: 5 login attempts/minute
BCrypt work factor ≥ 12
Security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, CSP
Dependency audit: dotnet list package --vulnerable, npm audit
Findings documented with severity and remediation

userStories.title: UX Review — All Screens
userStories.description: As a product owner, I want UX review of all screens for polished beta experience.
acceptanceCriteria: All 16+ pages reviewed: consistency, responsive, loading states, error states, empty states
Mobile (375px) verified on all pages
Dark theme verified: no broken colors, unreadable text
Financial values: R$ 1.234,56 with monospace
All forms have validation messages
All deletes have confirmation dialogs
All loading states use skeleton placeholders
Toast notifications for all CRUD
Navigation consistent: sidebar, breadcrumbs, back buttons
Issues filed as GitHub Issues
Angular build passes

acceptanceCriteria: Docs/deployment.md: VPS setup, env vars, Docker commands
Docs/environment-variables.md: all env vars with descriptions and defaults
Docs/troubleshooting.md: common issues and solutions
Docs/backup-restore.md: backup verification and restore procedures
.env.example in repo root with all required variables
docker-compose.prod.yml for production
